Working in an isolated git worktree of the AHBU portfolio project. RE-PASS for `sleep-staging` layer 20 — the user reports HMC PSG PhysioNet credentialed access is uncertain in timeline AND has provided an NSRR token (in `.env` as `NSRR_TOKEN=...`, gitignored — DO NOT print or commit the value). The locked protocol must be revised.

**Read first** (in the worktree):
- CLAUDE.md (Spec convention + Pilot vs headline)
- 10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md (your spec)
- 20-plan/sleep-staging/{approach,risk-register,protocol-lock,pilots-README}.md (current locked protocol)
- 10-pain-point/sleep-staging/methodology-critic.md (prior critic verdict pass-with-fixes)
- 30-implement/sleep-staging/code/README.md, requirements.txt
- 30-implement/sleep-staging/code/pilots/p1_hmc_access_check.py (gating pilot)

**The triggers:**
1. **HMC PSG access uncertain.** PhysioNet credentialed access requires DUA-equivalent signup; user reports it may take time. The current protocol has HMC as the primary scope (b) dataset (77 test subjects out of 154). Risk-register R-2 already names CAP Sleep as substitute (PhysioNet OPEN tier, no DUA, ~108 subjects with ECG channels, similar pathology mix).
2. **NSRR token available.** User has obtained an NSRR API token (`NSRR_TOKEN` in `.env`). Per NSRR protocol, token is issued AFTER DUA approval — so SHHS/MESA access is no longer behind a 4-week wait. The original 4-week kill criterion in R-1 no longer applies.

**Your job: revise both scopes.**

**Scope (b) substitution path:** evaluate CAP Sleep as primary HMC substitute. CAP Sleep (PhysioNet open: https://physionet.org/content/capslpdb/1.0.0/) — verify subject count, ECG channel availability, AASM stage labels availability. ~108 subjects per R-2. Or alternative open sources: ISRUC-Sleep, Dreem-DOD-H + Sleep-EDF combination, etc. Pick the best substitute with documented justification.

**Scope (a) NSRR-fast-path:** with token available, scope (a) (clinical-population stratified eval of pretrained stagers on NSRR SHHS/MESA) becomes immediately runnable instead of conditionally available. Move scope (a) from "secondary, conditional, 4-week kill" to "primary or co-primary headline" — at your discretion based on which framing best supports the headline question.

**Constraints:**
- Use Write tool, NOT Bash heredoc.
- DO NOT print or commit the NSRR_TOKEN value. Reference it as `os.environ['NSRR_TOKEN']` in code; document in approach.md that it lives in `.env` (gitignored).
- Confirm CAP Sleep / NSRR access details from official sources (use WebFetch on physionet.org/content/capslpdb/1.0.0/ and sleepdata.org for the dataset pages).
- ≤ 60 tool uses.

**Files expected** in worktree:
- `20-plan/sleep-staging/unlock-note-2026-05-03.md` (NEW) — explains both triggers, what changes, justification for chosen substitute(s)
- `20-plan/sleep-staging/protocol-lock.md` (OVERWRITE) — revised held-out partition + datasets + power story
- `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md` (EDIT) — dataset section, NSRR access via token, CAP Sleep substitution
- `20-plan/sleep-staging/risk-register.md` (EDIT) — R-1 (NSRR DUA) marked RESOLVED via token; R-2 (HMC access) marked TRIGGERED+RESOLVED via CAP substitution; new risks if any
- `20-plan/sleep-staging/pilots-README.md` (EDIT — P-1 needs to point at CAP not HMC)

Each file starts with `> **Spec:** ...` per spec convention.

Post a short summary on completion: chosen substitute for HMC + NSRR fast-path framing + revised headline + any new risks introduced by the substitution.

1 # AHBU — agent operating notes 2 3 How this project is structured and operated. For the WHY, read `00-vision/README.md`. For 30-second orientation, read `README.md`. 4 5 ## Architecture 6 7 ```mermaid 8 graph BT 9 PT1["Pain point A"] 10 PT2["Pain point B"] 11 PTn["Pain point …"] 12 IM["30 Implement
code · runs · shared substrate · compute · datasets"] 13 PL["20 Plan
design + interpret
(per admitted track)"] 14 PP["10 Pain-Point
discover + admission
(per candidate)"] 15 V["00 Vision
root: why + hard constraints"] 16 17 IM -- helps --> PL 18 PL -- helps --> PP 19 PP -- helps --> V 20 21 PT1 -. instance .-> PP 22 PT1 -. instance .-> PL 23 PT1 -. instance .-> IM 24 PT2 -. instance .-> PP 25 PT2 -. instance .-> PL 26 PTn -. instance .-> PP 27 28 style V fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#f59e0b 29 style IM fill:#e0f2fe,stroke:#0284c7 30 ``` 31 32 Solid arrows = help relations (Layered Endeavor Framework). A pain point exists as a folder inside whichever layers it has reached. Each layer also owns a `shared/` subfolder for layer-scoped substrate that doesn't belong to any single pain point. 33 34 ## Repo layout 35 36 ``` 37 00-vision/ 38 README.md # vision, hard constraints, stance 39 40 10-pain-point/ # discover + validate pain 41 README.md 42 / # one folder per investigated candidate 43 candidate.md # pain statement, evidence, gap-closing notes 44 admission.md # admission record (only once admitted) 45 real-pain-critic.md # critic pass on real-pain claim 46 shared/ 47 portfolio.md # registry: candidate / admitted / deferred / retired 48 validation-log.md # chronological log 49 selection-shortlist.md # historical (v1 rubric) 50 critic-shortlist.md # historical (v1 rubric) 51 critic-defensibility.md # historical (v1 rubric, now advisory) 52 53 20-plan/ # pain → technical: design + interpret 54 README.md 55 / # one folder per admitted track 56 approach.md # data, model, eval, ablations, uncertainty, novelty 57 risk-register.md # risks, mitigations, kill criteria, retire-cancel triggers 58 protocol-lock.md # pre-registered headline (frozen) 59 methodology-critic.md # critic at 20→30 boundary 60 pilots-README.md # pilot probes (dev-only, not pre-registered) 61 findings.md # post-headline interpretation 62 limitations.md # post-headline honest limitations 63 lessons.md # post-headline; promotion candidates for shared/ 64 shared/ 65 reuse-sketches/ # per-candidate cross-track-leverage sketches 66 67 30-implement/ # technical execution 68 README.md 69 compute.md # binding compute envelope + opt-in fallbacks 70 datasets.md # public dataset registry 71 / # one folder per running track (lazy) 72 code/ # reproducible scripts 73 runs/ # logs, metrics, configs 74 results.md # primary metrics, ablations, uncertainty 75 shared/ # lazy materialize 76 data/ # loaders, partition utilities, leakage detectors 77 eval/ # metrics, calibration, abstention, uncertainty wrappers 78 models/ # baselines (Riemannian, 1D-ResNet, classical pipelines) 79 80 bin/ # repo tooling (transcript sync, hooks) 81 transcripts/ # raw sub-agent JSONL (auto-synced pre-commit) 82 .claude/agents/ # agent personas 83 ``` 84 85 Each layer's `README.md` declares **layer expertise, mandate, knowledge, output, help target, layout, stance**. Don't reach across layers — flow goes through help relations. 86 87 ## Hard-constraint enforcement (layer routing) 88 89 The three hard constraints declared in `00-vision/README.md` are not all enforced at the same layer. Each is enforced where it can actually be verified: 90 91 | Constraint | Enforced at | Mechanism | 92 |---|---|---| 93 | Pain point real | Layer 10 (admission gate) | Real-pain critic pass + human checkpoint. Failing → don't admit. | 94 | Solution feasible | Layer 20 (plan) | Methodology design must fit our envelope. Failing → retire-cancel back to layer 10. NOT pre-judged at admission — pre-judging filters out creative framings before they get a real look. | 95 | Quality bar | Layer 20 (protocol pre-reg + analysis sign-off) + Layer 30 (execution) | Layer 20 designs the protocol that meets it; layer 30 executes; layer 20 interprets honestly post-headline. Failing at any → retire-cancel back to layer 10. | 96 97 Both `retire-completed` and `retire-cancelled` are valid portfolio exits. Both produce lessons. 98 99 ## Operating discipline 100 101 - **Critic pass** required at each help-boundary milestone (admission, protocol-lock, results sign-off, analysis sign-off). Use `.claude/agents/critic.md` persona via Agent tool or as a teammate. 102 - **Human checkpoint** at end of each meaningful chunk. Don't barrel through admission → planning → implementation without check-in. 103 - **Pain-point validation = required artifact** for every admission. No `20-plan//` exists without `10-pain-point//admission.md`. 104 - **Reuse first.** Methodology designers must scan `30-implement/shared/` before drafting `approach.md`. Promote eagerly to `shared/` once ≥1 plausible second consumer exists. 105 - **Use git.** Commit as you go. Tag milestones: `v0-vision`, `v1-shortlisted`, `v2--admitted`, `v3--protocol-locked`, `v4--results`, `v5--retired`. Branches encouraged for parallel tracks. 106 107 ### Within-layer agile, cross-layer gated 108 109 - Within a layer, iterate fast: cheap drafts, pilots, probes, scratch experiments. The cost of wrong inside a layer is low. 110 - At layer boundaries, gate strictly: critic pass + human checkpoint. The cost of advancing wrong work to the next layer is high. 111 112 This is the operational form of the vision's "efficiency over ceremony" stance. 113 114 ### Spec convention 115 116 Every work artifact in every layer starts with a one-line `> **Spec:** ` quote naming the upstream artifact whose mandate it serves. Makes the help relation traceable at file granularity (not only at layer-README granularity). Per-file mapping: 117 118 | File | Spec | 119 |---|---| 120 | `10-pain-point//candidate.md` | `10-pain-point/README.md` (candidate spec section) | 121 | `10-pain-point//admission.md` | `00-vision/README.md` (hard constraint #1: pain real and validated) + `10-pain-point/README.md` (validation rubric) | 122 | `10-pain-point//real-pain-critic.md` | `/candidate.md` (under review) + `10-pain-point/README.md` (rubric the critic applies) | 123 | `20-plan//{approach,risk-register,protocol-lock}.md` | `10-pain-point//admission.md` | 124 | `20-plan//pilots-README.md` | `/approach.md` | 125 | `20-plan//methodology-critic.md` | `/{approach,risk-register,protocol-lock}.md` (under review) + `10-pain-point//admission.md` (the spec layer 20 received) | 126 | `20-plan//{findings,limitations,lessons}.md` *(post-headline)* | `30-implement//results.md` + `/protocol-lock.md` | 127 | `30-implement//code/README.md` | `20-plan//approach.md` | 128 | `30-implement//code/pilots/p*.py` | `20-plan//pilots-README.md#P-N` (in module docstring; also surfaced in JSON `spec` field) | 129 | `30-implement//code/headline/*.py` | `20-plan//protocol-lock.md` | 130 | `30-implement//results.md` | `20-plan//protocol-lock.md` | 131 | `30-implement/shared///README.md` | the originating-track promotion event + the second-consumer that triggered promotion | 132 133 ### Pilot vs headline (per track, layer 20 + 30) 134 135 - **Pilots:** small, fast, exploratory. Listed in `20-plan//pilots-README.md`; executed by `30-implement//code/` against the dev split. Not pre-registered. 136 - **Headline:** the experiment that supports the track's primary claim. Pre-registered in `20-plan//protocol-lock.md` before it runs. Touches the held-out partition exactly once, in `30-implement//`. 137 138 Pilots feed methodology; headline feeds analysis. Don't conflate them. 139 140 ## Quality bar (cross-cutting, applies to every track) 141 142 - Honest held-out testing. Held-out touched once for the headline. 143 - Uncertainty reported on every metric. 144 - Ablations on load-bearing design choices. 145 - Failure modes characterized, not buried. 146 - No metric gaming, no cherry-picking, no hand-waving. 147 148 If meeting the quality bar with the chosen approach is impossible, that is itself a finding — surface it, don't paper over. 149 150 ## Resources 151 152 `30-implement/compute.md` — binding compute envelope (currently GTX 1650 4 GB) + track-specific opt-in fallbacks (e.g., Kaggle T4 for FM feature extraction). 153 `30-implement/datasets.md` — public dataset registry. 154 155 ## Agent team 156 157 `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` enabled in `.claude/settings.local.json`. Use **agent teams** (peer-to-peer + shared task list) for phases that benefit from cross-challenge / parallel exploration — admission gap-closing, methodology debate, parallel-track work, ablation runs. Use plain subagents (Agent tool) for one-shot lookups. 158 159 Defined personas in `.claude/agents/`: 160 161 - `pain-point-researcher` — surveys constituencies + literature for evidence of real pain. Operates in layer 10. 162 - `critic` — adversarial review at help boundaries. 163 - `methodologist` — designs the approach in layer 20; reuse-scan against `30-implement/shared/` first. 164 165 Spawn additional teammates (data-plumber, baseline-builder, ablation-runner, writer, shared-promoter) when work calls for it. 166 167 Env-var changes take effect on **next session**. Restart before agent-team commands become available. 168 169 ## Transcripts 170 171 Every sub-agent JSONL transcript is auto-synced from `~/.claude/projects///subagents/` into `transcripts/` via the `bin/sync-transcripts.ps1` script, wired as a git pre-commit hook by `bin/install-hooks.ps1`. Manifest at `transcripts/MANIFEST.md` maps agentId → persona/task. 172 173 ## Kickoff for fresh session 174 175 1. Read `00-vision/README.md` and the four layer READMEs. 176 2. Read `10-pain-point/shared/portfolio.md` for active state. 177 3. Pick up the next chunk per the active track's layer: 178 - At layer 10 → real-pain-researcher closes any open gaps in `/candidate.md`; critic does real-pain pass; admission record drafted. 179 - At layer 20 → methodologist drafts `approach.md` + `risk-register.md` + `protocol-lock.md`; critic at 20→30 boundary; pilots designed. 180 - At layer 30 → execute pilots first (dev-only); execute headline once locked; results to `/results.md`. 181 - Back at layer 20 → interpret in `findings.md` + `limitations.md` + `lessons.md`; critic on analysis sign-off. 182

3	# Admission record — sleep-staging
4	
5	- **Date:** 2026-05-02
6	- **Slug:** `sleep-staging`
7	- **Rubric version:** v2 (real-pain only at layer 10; feasibility / quality bar enforced at layers 20 / 30)
8	- **Verdict:** **admitted** (pass-with-fixes — Stone→Chinoy and Bakker→Choo author-name fixes applied in candidate.md)
9	- **Track dirs:** `20-plan/sleep-staging/` (next), `30-implement/sleep-staging/` (later)
10	
11	## Pain statement (one paragraph)
12	
13	Sleep staging at clinical and consumer scale is rate-limited by inter-rater disagreement (κ ≈ 0.76 overall, N1 κ ≈ 0.24), automated-stager performance collapse on edge populations (Korkalainen 2020 OSA-severity degradation 84.5 % → 76.5 %; pediatric bias), AASM v3 hypopnea-rule disputes that change scoring outcomes, and consumer wearable misclassification (30–50 % of REM and deep sleep wrong on common devices). Sleep technologists spend ~70 minutes per PSG manually scoring at $23–72/hr; clinicians manage downstream confusion when wearable stage estimates disagree with PSG. Constituencies feel this in time, money, and clinical decision quality.
14	
15	## Real-pain evidence (admission gate)
16	
17	≥ 2 independent evidence classes per sub-pain, all confirmed by critic pass with two author-name fixes applied:
18	
19	1. **Inter-rater ceiling.** Lee et al. 2022 meta-analysis — overall κ = 0.76; N1 κ = 0.24 (verified).
20	2. **Automated stager edge-case collapse.** Korkalainen 2020 — 84.5 % → 76.5 % accuracy as OSA severity increases (verified). Plus van Gorp 2023 + U-Sleep peds 2024 (pediatric bias).
21	3. **AASM v3 dispute.** Rosen & Auckley 2024 (JCSM commentary on hypopnea rule); Malhotra 2024 (PMC11063699, letter, separate paper); AASM v3 summary PDF.
22	4. **Wearable misclassification — multi-device PSG validation.** Chinoy et al., Sleep 2021 (PMC8120339) — most devices misclassify 30–50 % of REM and deep sleep (verified; previously misattributed to "Stone et al.", fixed 2026-05-02).
23	5. **Wearable misclassification — multicenter benchmark.** JMIR mHealth 2023 (PMC10654909) — best macro-F1 0.69, worst 0.26 across 349 k epochs (verified).
24	6. **Consumer device studies.** Chee et al., Sleep Med 2024 (Oura Gen3 + OSSA 2.0 multi-night); Oura systematic review 2025 (PMC12602993).
25	7. **Sleep-tech burden — timed observation.** Choo et al., Frontiers in Neurology 2023 (PMC9981786) — 4,243 s manual vs 42.7 s autoscore per PSG; 0.25 FTE savings at 750 patients/year (verified; previously misattributed to "Bakker et al.", fixed 2026-05-02).
26	8. **Sleep-tech burden — independent.** Holm et al., J Sleep Research 2026 (PMC12856104) — manual scoring "up to 2 h" per 8-h PSG; 14 % staging disagreement, 34.6 % respiratory-event disagreement, OSA severity differs in 66 % of cases by scorer (verified).
27	9. **Patient voice.** Baron 2017 case-series patient quotes (verified verbatim).
28	10. **Pediatric scoring difficulty.** Kevat / JCSM 2025 pediatric κ figures (verified).
29	
30	Independence threshold (≥ 2 sources per sub-claim) comfortably exceeded across all 5 sub-pains.
31	
32	## Constituency
33	
34	Sleep technologists (paid $23–72/hr per BLS), pediatric / geriatric sleep researchers, sleep clinicians (AASM v3 cohort), wearable users + their downstream clinicians. **Reachable** via AASM Learning Center, AAST workforce surveys, Sleep-EDF / NSRR / Dreem-DOD dataset maintainers, paper-author emails.
35	
36	## Critic pass
37	
38	`real-pain-critic.md` — verdict `pass-with-fixes`. Two major (Stone→Chinoy and Bakker→Choo author-name corrections — both fixed in candidate file 2026-05-02; underlying data confirmed accurate). Two minor (Rosen & Auckley DOI 10.5664/jcsm.10944 not independently verifiable due to TLS error — sub-pain still supported by Malhotra; AAST 2023 workforce-survey full-data inaccessible — Choo/Holm timed observation independently meets threshold). Neither minor blocks admission.
39	
40	## Advisory annotations (NOT gating — input to layer 20 / 30)
41	
42	**From gap-closing pass (researcher a922da):**
43	- Dreem-DOD-H/-O access path documented; CC-BY-4.0 license, registration friction confirmed but ≤ 1 week.
44	- Sleep-tech workforce burden quantified primary-source (Choo 2023, Holm 2026, AAST attrition).
45	- Pediatric scoring difficulty quantified primary-source (Kevat 2025).
46	- Direct named-technologist interview NOT obtained — inferential evidence (paid training market) cited; close in track life if reviewer needs voice.
47	
48	**From defensibility critic (a52506) — historical, advisory:**
49	- sleep-staging scope (a) clinical-population stratified eval of pretrained stagers: fragile-null on DUA-free data (Dreem-DOD-O n=55, 3–7× underpowered for OSA stratification). RECOVERY: NSRR DUA submitted day-one with 4-week kill criterion → defensible-null.
50	- sleep-staging scope (b) HRV-only EEG-less staging: defensible-null on HMC PSG (151 subjects) for ≥ 17 pp EEG-vs-HRV gap.
51	
52	**From reuse sketch (methodologist a6dd95):**
53	- Promotion candidates: `30-implement/shared/data/cohort_stratifier.py` (per-cohort eval splits), `30-implement/shared/data/hrv_features.py` (HRV pipeline — UNIQUE to this track), `30-implement/shared/eval/calibration.py` (overlaps ecg-ppg-realworld). Plausible 2nd consumers = ecg-ppg-realworld + cross-subject-eeg (calibration + cohort stratifier).
54	
55	## Open items to close in early track life
56	
57	- Confirm Rosen & Auckley DOI 10.5664/jcsm.10944 (currently TLS-error-blocked)
58	- AAST 2023 workforce survey full data (currently 404 — qualify as institutional position statement)
59	- Direct named-technologist or named-clinician individual-voice quote on scoring burden
60	- NSRR DUA submission on day one of methodology — gates the OSA-stratified scope feasibility
61	
62	## Human checkpoint
63	
64	- [x] Lead reviewed critic verdict and evidence — 2026-05-02
65	- [ ] Human approval to instantiate `20-plan/sleep-staging/`
66	
67	When human approves, instantiate `20-plan/sleep-staging/`, update `10-pain-point/shared/portfolio.md`, tag `v2-sleep-staging-admitted`.

1	# Reuse sketch — sleep-staging (pre-admission aid)
2	
3	Author: methodologist agent
4	Date: 2026-05-02
5	Status: admission-aid sketch only — not a locked methodology
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Working scope
10	
11	Narrowed per shortlist: calibration and cohort-stratified evaluation of a
12	pretrained sleep stager (U-Sleep or equivalent checkpoint) on a held-out
13	clinical population. Specifically: quantify ECE, per-stage reliability
14	diagrams, and per-AHI-band F1 on a dataset that is *not* in U-Sleep's
15	training set. HRV-only EEG-less staging on MESA is an optional second
16	experiment if NSRR DUA arrives. No retraining of U-Sleep from scratch.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## 2. Approach in three bullets
21	
22	- **Data.** Primary: Sleep-EDF-Cassette v1 (PhysioNet ODC-BY, ~78 subjects
23	  healthy adults, 2-channel EEG) for pipeline development; Dreem-DOD-H/O
24	  (CC-BY 4.0 with Dreem registration, ~80 subjects healthy + OSA) as
25	  out-of-distribution clinical probe. SHHS/MESA (NSRR DUA) pursued in
26	  parallel as stretch goal — if DUA takes >4 weeks, the sketch remains valid
27	  on Dreem-DOD alone. HMC PSG 2018 (PhysioNet, no DUA) as additional
28	  cross-cohort site.
29	
30	- **Model family.** Primary: frozen U-Sleep checkpoint (trained on 15,660
31	  PSGs; inference fits GTX 1650 easily — no backprop). Calibration head:
32	  temperature scaling fitted on a held-out validation subset of Dreem-DOD.
33	  Baseline: random forest on HRV + spectral features extracted via MNE-Python
34	  or NeuroKit2 (EEG-less or single-channel path). No foundation-model
35	  pretraining.
36	
37	- **Eval skeleton.** Primary metric: per-stage macro-F1 on Dreem-DOD test
38	  partition, held-out subjects. Secondary: ECE per stage, reliability
39	  diagrams, performance stratified by AHI band (none / mild / moderate /
40	  severe), comparison against the human-rater kappa ceiling (0.76 overall,
41	  0.24 N1). Bootstrap CI over subjects. Ablation: uncalibrated vs
42	  temperature-scaled stager; with vs without the Dreem cohort as in-domain
43	  fine-tuning data (measures calibration transfer gap).
44	
45	---
46	
47	## 3. Shared substrate — promotion side
48	
49	shared/ is currently empty; no existing components to consume. Promotion
50	candidates this track would contribute on success:
51	
52	- **`30-implement/shared/eval/calibration.py`** — same module as ecg-ppg-realworld sketch
53	  proposes (`brier_score`, `expected_calibration_error`,
54	  `reliability_diagram`). If ecg-ppg-realworld is admitted first, this track
55	  consumes it; if sleep-staging is first, this track promotes it.
56	
57	- **`30-implement/shared/eval/cohort_stratifier.py`** — `stratified_report(y_true,
58	  y_pred, y_prob, stratum_col, stratum_vals)` that produces per-stratum F1,
59	  ECE, and confusion matrix. Stratum column is arbitrary (AHI band, age
60	  decile, site, skin-tone proxy). Interface is domain-agnostic once the
61	  stratum metadata is provided. Any track with subgroup evaluation needs this.
62	
63	- **`30-implement/shared/eval/partition.py`** — `subject_disjoint_split(df, subject_col,
64	  test_frac, stratify_col, seed)`. Same utility as ecg-ppg-realworld sketch
65	  proposes; if both are promoted by different tracks they should be merged
66	  into a single module.
67	
68	- **`30-implement/shared/data/sleep_edf_loader.py`** — loader for Sleep-EDF-Cassette
69	  returning `(eeg_epochs, stage_labels, subject_metadata)` at 30-second
70	  granularity, compatible with MNE-Python Raw objects. Reference pattern for
71	  epoch-based biosignal datasets.
72	
73	- **`30-implement/shared/models/hrv_feature_pipeline.py`** — `extract_hrv_features(rr_intervals,
74	  window_sec, step_sec)` returning a feature matrix of time-domain and
75	  spectral HRV features via NeuroKit2. Reusable by any track working with
76	  cardiac inter-beat intervals (ecg-ppg-realworld, affective-state
77	  arousal-only scope, cross-subject-eeg sleep-staging sub-scope).
78	
79	---
80	
81	## 4. Plausible 2nd consumer
82	
83	**ecg-ppg-realworld.** The HRV feature pipeline (`hrv_feature_pipeline.py`)
84	is directly reusable for HRV-validity work on PTB-XL / PPG-DaLiA; the
85	calibration module is consumed as-is. The cohort stratifier applies to
86	site-stratified PTB-XL evaluation.
87	
88	**cross-subject-eeg.** The subject_disjoint_split utility applies directly;
89	the cohort_stratifier could stratify by paradigm (MI vs P300 vs SSVEP) or
90	hardware condition, which mirrors its stratum-column design.
91	
92	**affective-state (if admitted).** The HRV feature pipeline covers the
93	cardiac arm of affective-state feature extraction; the cohort stratifier
94	applies to cross-dataset evaluation.
95	
96	Multiple plausible 2nd consumers exist. The HRV pipeline is the most valuable
97	unique contribution of this track to shared/ because it is not clearly
98	produced by any other candidate on their own terms.
99	
100	---
101	
102	## 5. Cross-track leverage rating
103	
104	**High.**
105	
106	The HRV feature pipeline is a unique contribution no other candidate produces
107	naturally, and it is consumed by at least three of the four tracks. The
108	calibration and cohort-stratifier utilities duplicate what ecg-ppg-realworld
109	would also produce — meaning the leverage depends partly on sequencing: if
110	sleep-staging is second, it consumes rather than builds those; if it is first,
111	it contributes them. Either way the net leverage is high. The one liability is
112	the NSRR DUA lag, which can delay first results by 4-8 weeks on the SHHS/MESA
113	stretch goal, but the core sketch (Dreem-DOD + Sleep-EDF) is DUA-free.
114

1	> **Spec:** `10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md`
2	
3	# Risk register — sleep-staging
4	
5	**Track:** sleep-staging
6	**Date:** 2026-05-02
7	**Author:** methodologist agent
8	**Status:** draft — pending critic gate before layer 30
9	
10	Each risk has: description, probability (H/M/L), impact (H/M/L),
11	mitigation, and a concrete kill criterion. Kill criteria are threshold-
12	triggered: hitting one stops the affected arm immediately.
13	"Retire-cancel" means the whole track exits; otherwise only the
14	affected arm is cancelled.
15	
16	The track has two arms — (b) HRV-only primary and (a) pretrained-
17	stager conditional. Kill criteria that affect only arm (a) do NOT
18	cancel the track; arm (b) is the safety net.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## R-1 — NSRR DUA not submitted or not received in time (scope a)
23	
24	**Description:** The NSRR DUA for SHHS and MESA is not submitted on
25	day one of this layer, or is submitted but not approved within 4 weeks
26	of submission. Without SHHS or MESA, scope (a) OSA-stratification
27	headline is powered only by Dreem-DOD-O (n=55), which is 3–7x
28	underpowered for the primary claim (defensibility critic finding,
29	10-pain-point/shared/critic-defensibility.md §3).
30	
31	**Probability (non-submission):** L (flagged as required action;
32	tracked explicitly).
33	**Probability (submission but late approval):** M (NSRR documented
34	approval lag 1–4 weeks; can extend to 8 weeks if institutional sign-off
35	is required).
36	**Impact:** H for scope (a); zero for scope (b) — scope (b) is DUA-free.
37	
38	**Required first action (day one):**
39	Submit the NSRR DUA for SHHS and MESA immediately on the first day of
40	this layer. Log submission confirmation (email confirmation or NSRR
41	account DUA-status page screenshot) in
42	`30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/nsrr_dua_submission.txt`.
43	
44	**Mitigation:**
45	1. Submit DUA day one. Use institution email for fastest processing.
46	2. While waiting: execute all scope (b) work (weeks 1–4) and scope (a)
47	   pilot work on Dreem-DOD-O (n=55) with honest CI widths.
48	3. If DUA arrives by week 4: begin NSRR preprocessing; scope (a)
49	   headline runs weeks 6–8.
50	4. If DUA does not arrive by week 4 after submission: scope (a) is
51	   cancelled per the kill criterion below. Scope (b) headline proceeds
52	   as the sole track result.
53	
54	**Kill criterion:** If the NSRR DUA is NOT approved within 4 calendar
55	weeks of the confirmed submission date, cancel scope (a) OSA-
56	stratification headline. Scope (b) HRV-only headline proceeds
57	unaffected. Document the DUA timeline in results.md. The 4-week
58	clock starts from the date recorded in
59	`30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/nsrr_dua_submission.txt`.
60	
61	Note (m-4 fix): "4 weeks" is **4 calendar weeks from the confirmed
62	submission date** logged in `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/nsrr_dua_submission.txt`,
63	regardless of when in the layer that date falls. No proportional
64	adjustment.
65	
66	---
67	
68	## R-2 — HMC PSG access requires multi-week DUA or is unavailable
69	
70	**Description:** The HMC Sleep Staging Database (Hassan et al. 2023, doi:10.13026/533e-0n28)
71	is described as requiring PhysioNet credentialed access. At implementation
72	start, the actual access tier may be more restrictive than expected —
73	requiring a multi-week institutional DUA rather than a same-session
74	account creation.
75	
76	**Probability:** M (PhysioNet credentialed access is typically quick,
77	but specific datasets may have additional requirements not apparent from
78	the landing page).
79	**Impact:** H for scope (b) — HMC PSG is the primary scope (b) dataset.
80	If access is blocked, the primary arm has no data.
81	
82	**Mitigation:**
83	1. Verify HMC PSG access tier in week 1 before any downstream
84	   dependencies are built.
85	2. If multi-week DUA required: substitute with CAP Sleep database
86	   (PhysioNet, ~108 subjects with various pathologies including sleep
87	   apnea; similar PhysioNet access tier). The CAP dataset has ECG
88	   channels for HRV extraction.
89	3. If CAP Sleep also requires DUA: use Dreem-DOD-H (25 subjects) for
90	   a smaller-scale scope (b) pilot and re-evaluate feasibility.
91	   At n=25, the scope (b) headline is underpowered — see kill criterion.
92	
93	**Kill criterion:** If HMC PSG access requires a DUA that will not be
94	approved within 2 weeks, AND no substitute dataset with ≥ 75 subjects
95	with ECG channels is available DUA-free, retire-cancel the scope (b)
96	primary arm and retire-cancel the entire track unless scope (a) NSRR
97	data has already arrived. If scope (a) NSRR data is available, the
98	track may pivot to scope (a) only. Document the substitution attempt
99	in the retire-cancel note.
100	
101	---
102	
103	## R-3 — ECG channel absent or unusable in HMC PSG subjects
104	
105	**Description:** HMC PSG includes ECG, but individual recordings may
106	have ECG channels with severe noise, flat-line segments, or electrode
107	dropout that makes R-peak detection unreliable. Excessive ECG quality
108	exclusions may reduce the usable subject count below the 76-subject
109	test threshold.
110	
111	**Probability:** M (clinical PSG recordings routinely have ECG quality
112	issues; the exclusion rate in published HRV studies on PSG data is
113	typically 5–20 %).
114	**Impact:** M — reduces effective sample size.
115	
116	**Mitigation:**
117	1. Apply SQI threshold (> 30 % low-quality windows → exclude subject)
118	   as specified in preprocessing §Stage 1.
119	2. Report the exclusion count explicitly.
120	3. If exclusion reduces the test split below N=60 subjects: the
121	   power for the 17 pp gap test drops to ~75 % (from 80 %). Report
122	   the actual achieved power.
123	4. If exclusion reduces test split below N=40: the 17 pp gap claim is
124	   no longer adequately powered.
125	
126	**Kill criterion:** If ECG quality exclusions leave fewer than 40
127	usable test subjects, the primary scope (b) headline metric (3-class
128	macro-F1 EEG-vs-HRV comparison) is underpowered to detect the 17 pp
129	gap. In that case, either (a) substitute the CAP Sleep dataset to
130	supplement the subject count, or (b) report the available result with
131	an explicit underpowered caveat and reduce the claim scope accordingly.
132	This does not automatically trigger retire-cancel but the results
133	interpretation must be downgraded.
134	
135	---
136	
137	## R-4 — U-Sleep checkpoint unavailable or license-incompatible
138	
139	**Description:** The U-Sleep pretrained checkpoint (Perslev et al.
140	2021, https://github.com/perslev/U-Sleep) is unavailable, requires
141	login, or carries a license that prohibits usage on Dreem-DOD or
142	NSRR data.
143	
144	**Probability:** L–M (U-Sleep has a research license that permits
145	non-commercial use; the checkpoint is available via GitHub; however,
146	the original paper was trained on NSRR data among others, which may
147	create license conflicts for evaluation on NSRR data specifically).
148	**Impact:** H for scope (a) — U-Sleep is the primary pretrained stager.
149	Impact L for scope (b) — U-Sleep is only the EEG comparison model.
150	
151	**Mitigation:**
152	1. Download and verify U-Sleep checkpoint at implementation start.
153	   Record SHA-256 hash.
154	2. Verify license against Dreem-DOD and HMC PSG data agreements.
155	3. If U-Sleep is unavailable: substitute with DeepSleepNet (Supratak
156	   et al. 2017; weights available at https://github.com/akaraspt/deepsleepnet)
157	   or SleepTransformer (Phan et al. 2022; weights available via
158	   corresponding author on request or via HuggingFace). Document the
159	   substitution.
160	4. For scope (b) EEG comparison specifically: a simpler CNN baseline
161	   (1D-ResNet trained on Sleep-EDF dev split and tested on HMC PSG
162	   test split) can substitute U-Sleep. This is a weaker EEG baseline
163	   but avoids the pretrained-checkpoint dependency entirely.
164	
165	**Kill criterion:** If no pretrained EEG stager checkpoint can be
166	obtained with a compatible license, cancel the pretrained-stager
167	comparison from scope (a). Scope (b) EEG comparison falls back to
168	a dev-trained 1D-ResNet baseline — see approach.md for the fallback.
169	If the 1D-ResNet fallback also cannot be trained within the compute
170	envelope (R-5), retire-cancel scope (a) and proceed with scope (b)
171	alone.
172	
173	---
174	
175	## R-5 — U-Sleep inference exceeds 4 GB VRAM on GTX 1650
176	
177	**Description:** U-Sleep processes multi-channel EEG at 100 Hz in
178	30-second epochs. At batch=32, the activation memory may exceed the
179	3 GB safe threshold (leaving headroom for OS/CUDA).
180	
181	**Probability:** M (U-Sleep architecture is not trivially small;
182	the original implementation used A100 GPUs, though inference is
183	lighter than training).
184	**Impact:** M — would require batch-size reduction or Kaggle fallback.
185	
186	**Mitigation:**
187	1. Run U-Sleep VRAM probe (pilot P-1) on a single batch before any
188	   large-scale inference. Measure `torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated()`.
189	2. If VRAM > 3 GB at batch=32: reduce to batch=8 or batch=1 and
190	   re-check. Inference speed decreases linearly with batch size
191	   but remains feasible.
192	3. If VRAM > 3 GB even at batch=1: move U-Sleep inference to Kaggle
193	   T4 (16 GB VRAM, 9 hr/week). Extract features (probabilities) in
194	   a Kaggle notebook, download the probability arrays locally for
195	   evaluation.
196	
197	**Kill criterion:** If U-Sleep inference cannot be run locally at any
198	batch size, AND Kaggle T4 inference fails (quota exhausted or
199	environment conflict unfixable within 2 days), cancel the U-Sleep EEG
200	comparison from scope (a) and scope (b). The scope (b) EEG comparison
201	falls back to the dev-trained 1D-ResNet baseline (R-4 fallback). If
202	that also fails, scope (b) reports HRV-only RF results without a
203	direct EEG comparison, noting the gap claim cannot be quantified at
204	this resource level. This does not retire-cancel the track but
205	narrows the headline claim.
206	
207	---
208	
209	## R-6 — Dreem-DOD download too large for local storage
210	
211	**Description:** dodh.zip (21.9 GB) + dodo.zip (36.2 GB) = 58.1 GB.
212	Local storage may be insufficient.
213	
214	**Probability:** M (depends on disk; not verified at methodology lock).
215	**Impact:** M — affects scope (a) OOD probe; does not affect scope (b)
216	primary arm.
217	
218	**Mitigation:**
219	1. Check available local disk space before scheduling Dreem-DOD download.
220	2. If insufficient: use Kaggle Datasets to mount Dreem-DOD via the
221	   Zenodo URL in a notebook. Extract features (probability arrays)
222	   in the notebook; download only feature matrices locally (~MB scale).
223	3. If scope (a) OOD probe cannot run: drop it from the track work plan.
224	   Scope (b) is unaffected.
225	
226	**Kill criterion:** If Dreem-DOD is inaccessible both locally and via
227	Kaggle within week 2, drop the Dreem-DOD OOD probe from scope (a).
228	This does not trigger retire-cancel; scope (b) headline is unaffected.
229	Document the access failure.
230	
231	---
232	
233	## R-7 — HRV features are insufficient to discriminate N1 from N2
234	
235	**Description:** N1 is the hardest stage for both human raters (κ=0.24)
236	and EEG-based automated stagers (F1 ~0.40–0.53). HRV-only features
237	may completely fail to discriminate N1 from N2, producing N1 F1
238	near zero. This is an expected outcome, not a model failure — but it
239	must be reported, not hidden.
240	
241	**Probability:** H (N1 is characterized primarily by EEG vertex sharp
242	waves and slow eye movements, not by distinctive HRV dynamics; N1 HRV
243	is similar to light NREM in most subjects).
244	**Impact:** L — this is a valid informative result, not a fatal outcome.
245	
246	**Mitigation:**
247	1. Report N1 F1 explicitly in 5-class results. Do not collapse N1
248	   into a success metric.
249	2. Compare HRV-only N1 F1 to human-rater N1 κ = 0.24 explicitly.
250	   A result near κ=0.24 in F1-equivalent terms shows HRV-only is at
251	   the human rater ceiling for N1 — which is itself interesting.
252	3. If 5-class HRV-only N1 F1 < 0.10: note that N1 discrimination is
253	   essentially chance-level without EEG, and the 3-class collapse
254	   (N1+N2+N3 → NREM) is scientifically motivated, not a post-hoc fix.
255	
256	**Kill criterion:** None. N1 failure is a pre-specified expected outcome.
257	It does not trigger a cancel.
258	
259	---
260	
261	## R-8 — HRV-only staging matches EEG (scope b null result)
262	
263	**Description:** The Wilcoxon test finds no significant difference
264	between HRV-RF and U-Sleep-EEG macro-F1 on the 77 test subjects.
265	This is the "wearables don't need EEG" finding.
266	
267	**Probability:** L (published literature reports ~17 pp gap; at
268	HMC PSG this may be larger or smaller depending on pathology mix).
269	**Impact:** L — this is a pre-specified defensible-null outcome per
270	the defensibility critic. Both directions are informative.
271	
272	**Mitigation:**
273	1. Report the full result including CIs. A null result with narrow CIs
274	   is more informative than a positive result with wide CIs.
275	2. If null: frame as "HRV-only staging is not detectably inferior to
276	   EEG-based staging at this sample size and pathology mix; this has
277	   implications for EEG-less wearable deployment."
278	3. Ensure ablations are complete so the null cannot be attributed to
279	   a weak EEG baseline (compare U-Sleep vs other EEG baselines as
280	   a dev-split probe).
281	
282	**Kill criterion:** None. The null outcome does not trigger a cancel.
283	
284	---
285	
286	## R-9 — Held-out partition touched prematurely (protocol violation)
287	
288	**Description:** Any analysis — including "just looking" at summary
289	statistics — is performed on the 76-subject test split before the
290	single authorized evaluation run.
291	
292	**Probability:** L–M (easy to trigger inadvertently when debugging
293	code on a "full" dataset).
294	**Impact:** H — invalidates the honest-evaluation claim.
295	
296	**Mitigation:**
297	1. Store test-subject IDs in a separate config variable (`test_subjects`)
298	   distinct from dev subjects. During dev and pilot phases, only
299	   dev_subjects is loaded.
300	2. Before the headline run, manually verify that no prior result file
301	   in `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/` contains metrics computed
302	   on test-split subjects.
303	3. If a test-split subject's data was inadvertently loaded: document
304	   the violation in results.md immediately. Assess whether any model
305	   selection decision was influenced by test-split information.
306	
307	**Kill criterion:** If a model selection decision (choice of feature set,
308	choice of HRV window length, choice of RF hyperparameters) was made
309	after seeing test-split performance, retire-cancel the headline
310	experiment. The evaluation is no longer honest. This is a hard cancel
311	with no recovery path. Document transparently.
312	
313	---
314	
315	## R-10 — Stage label quality in HMC PSG (single-rater labels)
316	
317	**Description:** HMC PSG (PhysioNet 2018 CinC) staging labels were
318	produced by a single clinical site. Single-rater labels have inherent
319	noise (human kappa for overall sleep staging ~0.76; N1 kappa ~0.24).
320	A model trained on single-rater labels and evaluated on the same
321	label set may appear to perform better than it actually does, because
322	both training and testing share the same rater's systematic errors.
323	
324	**Probability:** H (single-rater is the norm for CinC challenge data).
325	**Impact:** M — inflates apparent accuracy by unknown amount, but the
326	paired EEG-vs-HRV comparison remains valid because both models share
327	the same label source.
328	
329	**Mitigation:**
330	1. Acknowledge single-rater label limitation explicitly in approach
331	   and results.
332	2. The primary claim (EEG-vs-HRV gap) is not affected by single-rater
333	   bias because both models are evaluated against the same labels;
334	   the gap reflects modality difference, not label quality.
335	3. Optionally cross-reference a small subset of HMC PSG labels against
336	   Dreem-DOD multi-rater labels for orientation (this is a dev-only
337	   probe, not a headline).
338	
339	**Kill criterion:** None. This is a documented limitation, not a
340	fatal flaw. It constrains the absolute-accuracy claim but not the
341	comparative gap claim.
342	
343	---
344	
345	## R-11 — Dreem-DOD multi-rater consensus labels — version compatibility
346	
347	**Description:** Dreem-DOD was released in 2020 with a specific label
348	format (JSON per subject, 5-rater consensus). The Zenodo 2025 re-release
349	(record 15900394) may have different file formats or label encoding
350	than the benchmark repo's code expects.
351	
352	**Probability:** M (re-releases sometimes change packaging).
353	**Impact:** M — delays preprocessing; fixable but takes time.
354	
355	**Mitigation:**
356	1. Download Dreem-DOD and run a label-format smoke test in week 2
357	   (pilot P-2) before building the full scope (a) eval pipeline.
358	2. If format changed: adapt the loader to the new format. The benchmark
359	   repo (github.com/Dreem-Organization/dreem-learning-open) has data
360	   loading utilities that can serve as reference.
361	
362	**Kill criterion:** If Dreem-DOD labels cannot be loaded within 2 days
363	of discovery, drop the Dreem-DOD OOD probe. Scope (b) unaffected.
364	
365	---
366	
367	## Summary table
368	
369	| ID | Risk | Arm | P | I | Kill criterion |
370	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
371	| R-1 | NSRR DUA not received in 4 weeks | (a) | M | H | Cancel scope (a) headline |
372	| R-2 | HMC PSG DUA required / inaccessible | (b) primary | M | H | Retire-cancel track if no substitute ≥75-subject DUA-free dataset exists |
373	| R-3 | ECG quality exclusions reduce N < 40 | (b) | M | M | Downgrade claim scope; may substitute CAP dataset |
374	| R-4 | U-Sleep checkpoint unavailable | (a)/(b) | L–M | H | Cancel pretrained-stager comparison; fall back to dev-trained 1D-ResNet for EEG comparison |
375	| R-5 | U-Sleep VRAM > 3 GB on GTX 1650 | (a)/(b) | M | M | Move to Kaggle T4; if T4 also fails, cancel EEG comparison, narrow scope (b) claim |
376	| R-6 | Dreem-DOD download impractical locally | (a) | M | M | Use Kaggle; if inaccessible, drop Dreem-DOD OOD probe |
377	| R-7 | HRV N1 F1 near zero | (b) | H | L | None — valid pre-specified outcome |

12	## Scope decision
13	
14	Two scopes were evaluated by the defensibility critic prior to admission:
15	
16	**(a) Clinical-population stratified evaluation of pretrained stagers.**
17	Fragile-null on DUA-free data alone (Dreem-DOD-O n=55, 3–7x underpowered
18	for OSA stratification per the defensibility-critic power analysis).
19	Recoverable to defensible-null only if NSRR DUA is submitted on day one
20	of this layer and arrives within 4 weeks.
21	
22	**(b) HRV-only EEG-less staging.**
23	Defensible-null at HMC PSG scale (154 subjects). Independently feasible
24	with no administrative dependency. Both EEG-parity (supports wearable
25	deployment) and EEG-superiority (defines performance ceiling) are
26	informative outcomes.
27	
28	**Decision: pursue BOTH scopes in parallel sub-arms.** Scope (b) is
29	the primary arm — it is self-contained, has no external dependencies,
30	and fits inside the compute envelope trivially. Scope (a) is a
31	conditional stretch arm: the NSRR DUA is submitted on day one per the
32	defensibility critic's required action, and scope (a) work begins only
33	in week 4 after the DUA arrives; if the DUA does not arrive within
34	4 weeks of submission, scope (a) is dropped and scope (b) stands alone
35	as the track headline. This parallel structure ensures the track always
36	has a defensible primary result regardless of DUA timing.
37	
38	**Justification for not choosing scope (a) alone:** If the DUA does not
39	arrive in time, a scope-(a)-only track has no defensible headline.
40	Scope (b) guarantees a result.
41	
42	**Justification for not dropping scope (a) entirely:** The clinical-
43	population stratification question is the higher-impact contribution;
44	if the DUA arrives in time, executing scope (a) on SHHS/MESA elevates
45	the track's reach substantially. The marginal cost of submitting the
46	DUA and beginning conditional scope-(a) preparation is low.

340	## Preprocessing
341	
342	### Scope (b) — HRV feature pipeline (primary arm)
343	
344	This pipeline operates on the ECG channel of HMC PSG.
345	
346	**Stage 1: ECG loading and quality check**
347	
348	Software: MNE-Python 1.6.x, NeuroKit2 >= 0.2.0.
349	
350	1. Load the ECG channel from each subject's EDF file using
351	   `mne.io.read_raw_edf(preload=True)`.
352	2. Check sampling rate: HMC PSG ECG is nominally sampled at >= 256 Hz.
353	   If < 200 Hz, flag the recording and report the fraction flagged.
354	3. Compute signal-quality index (SQI): using NeuroKit2
355	   `nk.ecg_quality()`, flag 30-second windows with SQI < 0.5.
356	   Report the fraction of flagged windows per subject.
357	4. Subjects with > 30 % of windows flagged are excluded from scope (b)
358	   analysis (quality-exclusion criterion; logged, not hidden).
359	
360	**Stage 2: R-peak detection**
361	
362	1. `nk.ecg_peaks(ecg_cleaned, sampling_rate=fs)` using the "pantompkins"
363	   method as default. Record detector type.
364	2. Compute R-R interval sequence in milliseconds.
365	3. Apply ectopic-beat correction: `nk.hrv_sanitize()` or equivalent
366	   outlier removal (R-R intervals outside [300, 2000] ms flagged as
367	   ectopic, replaced by linear interpolation). Report ectopic fraction.
368	
369	**Stage 3: HRV feature extraction**
370	
371	Call `hrv_features.extract_hrv_features(rr_ms, window_sec=300,
372	step_sec=30)` per subject. The 300-second (5-minute) window is the
373	standard for spectral HRV analysis (Task Force 1996; gives frequency
374	resolution sufficient for LF/HF decomposition). Step size = 30 seconds
375	= epoch length.
376	
377	Features per 30-second epoch:
378	- Time-domain: mean_rr, sdnn, rmssd, pnn50
379	- Spectral (after cubic spline resampling at 4 Hz):
380	  lf_power (0.04–0.15 Hz), hf_power (0.15–0.40 Hz), lf_hf_ratio,
381	  vlf_power (0.003–0.04 Hz), total_power
382	- Nonlinear: sd1, sd2 (Poincare plot axes)
383	Total: 11 features per epoch.
384	
385	Rationale for 11 features: sufficient to capture the autonomic HRV
386	dynamics known to differentiate wake/NREM/REM (HF power increases
387	in NREM; LF/HF increases in REM; mean HR decreases in NREM, rises
388	in REM). Keeping the feature set small avoids overfitting on 75 dev
389	subjects.
390	
391	**Stage 4: Epoch alignment**
392	
393	Call `hrv_features.align_hrv_to_epochs(hrv_df, epoch_starts_sec)`
394	using the stage annotation file's epoch boundary timestamps. Produces
395	one feature vector per 30-second sleep epoch, aligned with stage label.
396	
397	**Stage 5: Class balance check**
398	
399	Per subject: report epoch counts per stage (W, N1, N2, N3, REM).
400	Expected class distribution in mixed-pathology dataset: N2 ~50 %,
401	N1 ~5 %, N3 ~15 %, REM ~20 %, W ~10 %. If any subject has 0 epochs
402	for a given stage, note it; do not impute. Report class balance per
403	split.
404	
405	### Scope (a) — pretrained stager inference pipeline (conditional)
406	
407	This pipeline requires EEG. Used on Sleep-EDF (dev), Dreem-DOD (OOD
408	probe), and NSRR data (headline, if DUA arrives).
409	
410	**Stage 1: EEG loading**
411	
412	1. Load EEG channels (C3-A2 or equivalent) via MNE-Python.
413	2. For Sleep-EDF: Fpz-Cz as single EEG channel (matches U-Sleep
414	   single-channel mode).
415	3. For Dreem-DOD: use the channel mapping provided in the Dreem
416	   benchmark repo (github.com/Dreem-Organization/dreem-learning-open).
417	4. Sampling rate: resample to 100 Hz (U-Sleep's required input rate)
418	   using MNE anti-aliasing filter.
419	
420	**Stage 2: Epoch extraction**
421	
422	30-second epochs as required by AASM staging protocol. Label source:
423	annotation files (Sleep-EDF: EDF+ annotation channel; Dreem-DOD:
424	JSON annotation files per subject).
425	
426	**Stage 3: Normalization**
427	
428	Normalize each epoch: subtract channel mean, divide by channel SD.
429	U-Sleep expects normalized input.
430	
431	**Stage 4: Pretrained stager inference**
432	
433	Primary: U-Sleep checkpoint (Perslev et al. 2021). The U-Sleep
434	inference API accepts a numpy array of normalized EEG epochs and
435	returns per-epoch probability distributions over 5 stages.
436	See https://github.com/perslev/U-Sleep for API.
437	
438	Compute: frozen inference on GTX 1650 is feasible for ~150 subjects
439	× ~1,000 epochs = ~150,000 epochs. At batch=32, each forward pass
440	is approximately 0.1 s. Total: ~500 s / ~8 minutes per subject
441	dataset. Full Dreem-DOD: ~10 hours on GPU. Feasible; see compute
442	budget below.
443	
444	**Stage 5: Calibration head (scope a only)**
445	
446	Temperature scaling on a held-out dev subset (Dreem-DOD-H, 25 subjects
447	— these subjects are NOT in the test set for scope (a)).
448	Fit a single temperature parameter T on the dev subset logits using
449	NLL minimization. Then report ECE before and after temperature scaling
450	to quantify calibration improvement. This uses
451	`calibration.multiclass_ece()` and `calibration.reliability_diagram()`.
452	
453	**Distribution-shift caveat (m-6 fix per methodology-critic):** Dreem-DOD-H
454	is 25 healthy subjects with the Dreem headband acquisition system; NSRR
455	SHHS / MESA are clinical-population PSGs with different acquisition
456	hardware, demographics, and pathology mix. Temperature scaling fit on
457	Dreem-DOD-H may be ineffective on NSRR test data (calibration is itself
458	non-transportable across distributions). Acknowledged limitation; if the
459	NSRR DUA arrives, RE-FIT the calibration head on a small NSRR dev subset
460	before applying to NSRR test (carve out ~10 % of NSRR for this).
461

463	
464	## Model family
465	
466	### Scope (b) — HRV-only classifier (primary arm)
467	
468	**Model A (primary): Random Forest on 11 HRV features**
469	
470	- Library: scikit-learn RandomForestClassifier.
471	- Hyperparameters: n_estimators=200, max_depth=None (let trees grow),
472	  class_weight="balanced" (to handle N1 class imbalance).
473	- Rationale: Random Forest is the canonical HRV-based sleep staging
474	  classifier (Fonseca et al. 2017; Radha et al. 2019 on 4-class HRV
475	  staging). It is interpretable via feature importance (which HRV
476	  features matter per stage), robust to the small-N regime (75 dev
477	  subjects), and does not require GPU. Feature importance is a
478	  secondary output of scientific interest.
479	- Training: fit once on the 75 dev subjects' epoch-level feature
480	  vectors and stage labels. No per-subject retraining — this is a
481	  population-level model.
482	- Test: evaluate on the 76 held-out test subjects, subject-disjoint.
483	
484	**Model B (primary comparison — EEG baseline for gap quantification):**
485	
486	EEG-based stager on HMC PSG — required to quantify the EEG-vs-HRV
487	performance gap (the primary scientific question).
488	
489	Option: U-Sleep frozen inference on the HMC PSG EEG channels.
490	The U-Sleep model was trained on 15,660 PSGs from 16 studies. HMC PSG
491	is not in U-Sleep's declared training corpus (per the published
492	supplementary). Confirm this via a dataset-overlap check before the
493	headline run (using the `partition.py` / manual audit; leakage_audit.py
494	from cross-subject-eeg can be adapted here).
495	
496	U-Sleep inference is run on the same 77 test subjects, same epochs.
497	This gives a paired comparison: for each test subject, both HRV-only-RF
498	accuracy and U-Sleep-EEG accuracy are available. The gap is the primary
499	metric.
500	
501	**Model C (baseline lower bound): Majority-class classifier**
502	
503	Predict N2 for all epochs (majority class, ~50 % of epochs).
504	This is the naive baseline. Any model that does not beat majority-class
505	on macro-F1 is not doing useful work.
506	
507	**Model D (exploratory, dev-split only): LSTM on HRV sequence**
508	
509	A small LSTM (1 layer, 64 hidden units, input = 11 HRV features per
510	epoch, sequence = 30 consecutive epochs) trained on dev subjects.
511	This tests whether temporal context (sleep cycles) improves HRV-only
512	staging beyond the epoch-wise Random Forest. On 75 dev subjects at
513	~1,000 epochs each = 75,000 training examples: a 1-layer LSTM with
514	64 units has ~37,000 parameters, training in minutes on CPU. This is
515	a pilot probe (P-5), not a pre-registered model.
516	
517	### Scope (a) — pretrained stager evaluation (conditional)
518	
519	No new model is trained. Scope (a) contributes:
520	1. U-Sleep inference on Dreem-DOD and (if DUA arrives) SHHS/MESA.
521	2. Calibration head (temperature scaling) fit on dev.
522	3. Per-AHI-band, per-stage F1 evaluation using `cohort_stratifier.py`.
523	
524	### Summary table
525	
526	| Model | Arm | GPU? | VRAM | Role |
527	|---|---|---|---|---|
528	| Random Forest (11 HRV features) | (b) | no | 0 | primary scope (b) |
529	| U-Sleep frozen inference (EEG) | (b) comparison / (a) | yes | ~1–2 GB | EEG gap baseline + scope (a) eval |
530	| Majority-class classifier | (b) | no | 0 | lower bound |
531	| LSTM on HRV sequence | (b) exploratory | CPU/GPU | < 0.5 GB | dev-only pilot |

533	---
534	
535	## Evaluation protocol
536	
537	### Structure
538	
539	Subject-disjoint throughout. Each subject appears in exactly one split
540	(dev or test). Within each split, evaluation is at the epoch level
541	(30-second windows), but statistical tests are performed at the subject
542	level (averaging over epochs per subject first, then comparing subjects).
543	
544	**3-class vs 5-class decision:**
545	Primary scope (b) evaluation uses 3-class labels (Wake / NREM / REM)
546	for power reasons (N=77 test subjects provides adequate power for a
547	17 pp 3-class accuracy gap; 5-class is reported secondarily with honest
548	CI widths). The 5-class result is informative but the headline claim is
549	anchored to 3-class macro-F1.
550	
551	Rationale: NREM consolidation (N1+N2+N3 → NREM) reflects how HRV
552	signals actually vary — the HRV signature of N1 vs N2 is subtle;
553	the HRV signature of NREM vs REM is strong. 5-class precision captures
554	the N1/N2/N3 detail but is not the primary axis of the HRV question.
555	
556	**Cross-subject evaluation (not cross-session):**
557	One model trained on dev subjects, evaluated on test subjects. This is
558	cross-subject generalization, which is the relevant question for
559	deployment (a wearable algorithm must work without per-user training).
560	
561	### Metrics
562	
563	**Primary metric (scope b):**
564	3-class macro-F1 (Wake / NREM / REM) on 76 held-out test subjects.
565	Reported as: "macro-F1 = X.XX (95 % CI: Y.YY–Z.ZZ, N=77 subjects)".
566	
567	**Primary comparison metric (scope b):**
568	EEG-vs-HRV gap = U-Sleep macro-F1 minus HRV-RF macro-F1 on the same
569	77 test subjects. This is a paired comparison.
570	
571	**Secondary metrics:**
572	- 5-class per-stage F1 (W, N1, N2, N3, REM) with 95 % CI — reported,
573	  not used for headline claim.
574	- 5-class confusion matrix — reported per model.
575	- AHI-stratified 3-class macro-F1 (none / mild / moderate / severe)
576	  — reported via `cohort_stratifier.stratified_report()`. For scope (b)
577	  this characterizes whether HRV-only staging degrades more for OSA
578	  patients (expected: yes, because OSA disrupts HRV dynamics).
579	- Per-stage feature importance (RF) — top HRV features per stage class.
580	- ECE per stage for U-Sleep (scope a) — using `calibration.multiclass_ece()`.
581	
582	### Cross-subject vs within-subject
583	
584	This study uses **cross-subject** evaluation exclusively. The population-
585	level model (RF trained on 75 dev subjects) is evaluated on 76 unseen
586	test subjects. Within-subject performance is not reported as a headline
587	because it is not the deployment-relevant scenario. Within-subject RF
588	(fit per subject using 80 % of that subject's epochs) is a dev-only
589	probe (P-4) to establish the within-subject ceiling.
590	
591	### Statistical testing
592	
593	**Paired test for EEG-vs-HRV gap:**
594	Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test (one-sided: H1 = EEG accuracy > HRV
595	accuracy), across 77 test subjects. Each subject yields one accuracy
596	score per model; pairing controls for subject-level difficulty variation.
597	Significance threshold: p < 0.05 (single test, no multiplicity
598	correction needed for the primary comparison).
599	Effect size: Cohen's d (paired).
600	
601	**Interpretation rule:**
602	- p < 0.05 AND d >= 0.5: "EEG significantly and substantially
603	  outperforms HRV-only; EEG is necessary for clinical-grade staging."
604	- p < 0.05 AND d < 0.5: "EEG statistically outperforms HRV-only but
605	  the gap is modest; HRV-only may be adequate for some use cases."
606	- p >= 0.05: "No statistically significant difference between EEG
607	  and HRV-only; HRV-only is not detectably worse than EEG at this
608	  sample size."
609	
610	All three outcomes are pre-specified and informative per the
611	defensibility-critic analysis.
612	
613	---
614	
615	## Ablations
616	
617	All ablations run on the dev split only.
618	
619	**Ablation 1: Feature subset ablation (time-domain only vs spectral only vs all)**
620	
621	Hypothesis: spectral features (LF/HF) carry the majority of
622	discriminative information for REM vs NREM. If accuracy with spectral-
623	only features is within 3 pp of full feature accuracy, and accuracy
624	with time-domain-only features drops > 10 pp, the spectral features
625	are the load-bearing HRV signal. This ablation directly addresses
626	"which HRV features matter" — a clinically meaningful sub-question
627	about wearable signal requirements.
628	
629	Procedure: train RF with (a) all 11 features, (b) 4 time-domain only,
630	(c) 5 spectral only, (d) 2 nonlinear only. Evaluate on dev split LOSO.
631	
632	**Ablation 2: Window length sensitivity (60 s vs 150 s vs 300 s)**
633	
634	Hypothesis: HRV spectral features require a minimum window of ~5 minutes
635	for reliable LF/HF estimation. Shorter windows (60–150 s) will
636	degrade spectral feature quality and reduce staging accuracy.
637	This tests whether the standard 5-minute spectral HRV window is
638	load-bearing or whether a 60-second window (lower latency, more
639	practical for wearables) is adequate.
640	
641	Procedure: compute HRV features at window lengths 60, 150, 300 s;
642	train separate RFs; compare macro-F1 on dev split.
643	
644	**Ablation 3: Ectopic correction on/off**
645	
646	Hypothesis: ectopic R-R intervals inflated RMSSD and pNN50 values
647	degrade classifier performance, particularly in OSA subjects where
648	ectopic beats are more common. Ectopic correction is load-bearing for
649	OSA subjects.
650	
651	Procedure: train one RF with ectopic correction and one without.
652	Compare accuracy stratified by AHI band on the dev split.
653	
654	**Ablation 4: 3-class vs 5-class target (scope b)**
655	
656	Hypothesis: HRV-only staging achieves reasonable performance on 3-class
657	(Wake/NREM/REM) but fails on N1 specifically. This would replicate the
658	N1 difficulty seen in EEG-based models (N1 F1 ~0.35–0.40 in literature)
659	and establish that N1 is hard regardless of modality.
660	
661	Procedure: train RF on 5-class labels; compare 5-class per-stage F1
662	to 3-class macro-F1 and to the human-rater kappa ceiling (N1 κ=0.24).
663	
664	---
665	
666	## Uncertainty reporting
667	
668	- **Bootstrap CI:** All macro-F1 means reported with 95 % bootstrap CI
669	  (n=2000 resamples, sampling with replacement at the subject level,
670	  fixed random_state=42). Subject-level resampling is correct because
671	  epochs within a subject are not exchangeable.
672	- **Multi-seed:** Random Forest uses random_state=42 for reproducibility.
673	  Report RF performance with 5 additional random seeds (43–47) on the
674	  dev set to confirm training-run variance is negligible (< 0.5 pp
675	  across seeds). Do not report multi-seed on the test split.
676	- **Reporting format:** "X.XX (95 % CI: Y.YY–Z.ZZ, N=77 test subjects)"
677	  for all headline numbers. Never report a point estimate without CI.
678	- **CI width as evidence:** If the CI width is > 15 pp, the result is
679	  inconclusive at this sample size and must be stated as such, not
680	  framed as a finding.
681

729	## Novelty and exploration notes
730	
731	**What is genuinely novel:**
732	- An **explicit, pre-registered, same-harness** paired EEG-vs-HRV staging
733	  comparison on HMC PSG (m-7 fix per methodology-critic — narrowed claim).
734	  Fonseca 2017 and Radha 2019 both ran HRV-only staging on PSG datasets
735	  that also contain EEG, so an implicit modality comparison was available
736	  in those works; the novel element here is the explicit same-harness
737	  pre-registered paired test with CI-width-led headline framing — not
738	  the absence of any prior modality comparison.
739	- Calibration evaluation on a pretrained stager (U-Sleep) applied to
740	  out-of-distribution data (Dreem-DOD OSA cohort). U-Sleep calibration
741	  on clinical-edge populations is not reported in the published U-Sleep
742	  papers.
743	- The AHI-stratified HRV staging accuracy decomposition: does HRV-only
744	  staging degrade more steeply than EEG-based staging as OSA severity
745	  increases? The defensibility critic identifies this as the informative
746	  sub-question that can be answered even at n=55 (Dreem-DOD-O) with
747	  honest CI widths.
748	
749	**What is standard and intentionally so:**
750	- Random Forest on HRV features: the canonical HRV sleep staging
751	  baseline (Fonseca 2017, Radha 2019). Using it is disciplined.
752	- U-Sleep as the EEG baseline: the best publicly available pretrained
753	  stager with open weights.
754	- Bootstrap CI at the subject level: correct uncertainty quantification
755	  for this design.
756	- 30-second epochs: AASM standard.
757	
758	**What is exploratory (pilot probes, not pre-registered):**
759	- LSTM on HRV sequence (P-5): tests temporal context benefit.
760	- U-Sleep temperature calibration improvement magnitude (P-3).
761	- Within-subject RF ceiling vs cross-subject RF gap (P-4).
762	- Scope (a) Dreem-DOD AHI-stratified ECE curves (P-2): framing
763	  exercise for the NSRR headline.

36	## 1. Headline experiment (pre-registered)
37	
38	**Scope b: paired HRV-only vs EEG staging comparison on HMC PSG.**
39	
40	The headline experiment answers: does HRV-only staging (Random Forest
41	on 11 HRV features) perform detectably differently from EEG-based
42	staging (U-Sleep frozen inference) when evaluated cross-subject on
43	the same population?
44	
45	This is a two-arm evaluation:
46	- Arm 1: HRV-only RF, trained on 75 dev subjects, tested on 76 held-out
47	  test subjects.
48	- Arm 2: U-Sleep pretrained EEG stager (frozen), evaluated on the same
49	  76 held-out test subjects (no re-training on HMC PSG).
50	
51	Both arms produce a 3-class prediction (Wake / NREM / REM) for each
52	30-second epoch. Macro-F1 is computed per subject; subjects are the
53	unit of statistical analysis.
54	
55	Scope (a) (OSA-stratified eval on NSRR data) is NOT part of this
56	locked headline. If the NSRR DUA arrives and scope (a) runs, it

58	headline run. The scope (b) headline is independent of scope (a).

87	## 3. Held-out partition definition (FROZEN)
88	
89	### Dataset
90	
91	**HMC PSG (HMC Sleep Staging Database (Hassan 2023) dataset),**
92	as accessed at implementation start. Exact URL:
93	https://physionet.org/content/hmc-sleep-staging/1.0.0/
94	The labeled training set (154 subjects with PSG + stage annotations)
95	is used.

97	### Subject split
98	
99	- **Dev subjects:** 75 subjects, selected by stratified random split
100	  (stratified by AHI category: none / mild / moderate / severe OSA,
101	  based on available metadata; if AHI is unavailable for all subjects,
102	  stratify by epoch count quartile as a proxy for recording quality).
103	- **Test subjects:** 77 subjects (the remaining 154 - 78), selected
104	  by the same stratified split.
105	- **Split seed:** random_state=42.
106	- **Split procedure:** `subject_disjoint_split(all_subjects, test_fraction=0.50,
107	  stratify_col=ahi_band, random_state=42)` (from partition.py or
108	  local implementation with same interface).
109	- **Split validation:** `validate_partition({"dev": dev_subjects,
110	  "test": test_subjects})` must pass (no subject overlap) before
111	  any experiment runs. Logged in
112	  `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/partition_audit.txt`.
113	
114	The exact subject-ID lists for dev and test splits are recorded in
115	`30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/partition_definition.json` at the time
116	of split creation, before any model training or inference begins.
117	This file is committed to git before week 1 experiments start.
118	
119	### Stage labels
120	
121	5-class AASM (W, N1, N2, N3, REM) from HMC PSG annotation files.
122	For the headline metric, map to 3-class: W=Wake, {N1, N2, N3}=NREM,
123	REM=REM.

125	### What counts as "touching the held-out split"
126	
127	The held-out split is touched when any of the following occurs:
128	- Test-subject file paths are passed to any data-loading function.
129	- Test-subject ECG or EEG data is read into memory.
130	- Test-subject epoch features or predictions are computed.
131	- Test-split metrics are computed or viewed.
132	
133	Checking that test-subject files exist on disk does NOT count.
134	
135	The single authorized evaluation run must be preceded by the

140	## 4. Primary metric (pre-registered)
141	
142	**Primary metric:** 3-class macro-F1 (Wake / NREM / REM) on
143	76 held-out test subjects, computed as follows:
144	
145	1. For each test subject s, compute per-epoch predictions from
146	   both HRV-RF and U-Sleep.
147	2. Map 5-class predictions to 3-class (N1, N2, N3 → NREM).
148	3. Compute macro-F1 per subject (macro = unweighted mean of
149	   per-class F1 over Wake, NREM, REM).
150	4. Report the mean across subjects with 95 % bootstrap CI
151	   (n=2000 resamples at the subject level, random_state=42).
152	
153	Unit: F1 score (0.0–1.0). Reported as: "macro-F1 = X.XX
154	(95 % CI: Y.YY–Z.ZZ, N=77 test subjects)".
155	
156	**Primary comparison metric:** EEG-vs-HRV gap =
157	U-Sleep-macro-F1 minus HRV-RF-macro-F1, paired by subject.
158	This delta is the headline quantity.
159	
160	**Secondary metrics (reported but not primary):**
161	- 5-class per-stage F1 (W, N1, N2, N3, REM) with 95 % CI.
162	- 5-class confusion matrix per model.
163	- AHI-stratified 3-class macro-F1 (if AHI data is available for
164	  sufficient subjects) via `cohort_stratifier.stratified_report()`.
165	- RF feature importance (top HRV features per class).
166	- Majority-class baseline macro-F1 (lower bound).
167	
168	---
169	
170	## 5. Statistical test (pre-registered)
171	
172	**Test:** Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test, one-sided.
173	H1: U-Sleep (EEG) macro-F1 > HRV-RF macro-F1, per subject.
174	
175	**Significance threshold:** p < 0.05 (single primary test;
176	no multiplicity correction — only one pre-registered comparison).
177	
178	**Effect size:** Cohen's d (paired).
179	
180	**Interpretation rule (applied at analysis time):**
181	
182	Case A: p < 0.05 AND d >= 0.5:
183	"EEG significantly and substantially outperforms HRV-only staging
184	(p < 0.05, d >= 0.5). EEG provides substantial additional information
185	beyond autonomic HRV dynamics for sleep staging at this population."
186	
187	Case B: p < 0.05 AND 0.2 <= d < 0.5:
188	"EEG statistically outperforms HRV-only staging (p < 0.05) but the
189	effect size is moderate (d = X.X). The absolute performance gap is
190	small enough that HRV-only may be adequate for low-precision wearable
191	applications."
192	
193	Case C: p < 0.05 AND d < 0.2:
194	"EEG outperforms HRV-only by a statistically significant but
195	practically negligible margin. HRV-only staging is effectively at
196	EEG parity for practical purposes at this population and threshold."
197	
198	Case D: p >= 0.05:
199	"No statistically significant difference between EEG-based and
200	HRV-only staging at N=77 (p = X.XX). This is a defensible-null
201	result: either the true gap is below the detection threshold at this
202	sample size, or HRV-only staging is genuinely at EEG parity in this
203	population."
204	
205	All four cases are pre-specified. The case label is determined by the
206	test result; no cherry-picking of framing post-hoc.
207	
208	---
209	
210	## 6. Decision rule: what counts as the headline result (pre-registered)
211	
212	The headline result is the full set of evaluation outputs on the
213	held-out test split, evaluated exactly once. It is "real" if:
214	
215	**Pre-run checklist (all must be true before touching the test split):**
216	- [ ] `partition_definition.json` committed to git with dev/test
217	      subject lists before any model training.
218	- [ ] `partition_audit.txt` shows `validate_partition()` passed.
219	- [ ] RF trained on dev subjects only (training confirmed by code
220	      inspection before headline run).
221	- [ ] U-Sleep checkpoint version recorded in requirements.txt.
222	- [ ] **U-Sleep training-data overlap audit run** (m-5 fix): confirm
223	      HMC Sleep Staging Database is NOT in U-Sleep's documented
224	      pre-training corpus (Perslev et al. — 16 studies, 15,660 PSGs;
225	      check the published list). If HMC is in pretrain, the EEG-vs-HRV
226	      paired comparison is contaminated and the headline must redesign
227	      (e.g., evaluate on a held-out cohort confirmed absent from
228	      U-Sleep training, or substitute a different EEG stager).
229	      Audit result logged in `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/usleep_overlap_audit.txt`.
230	- [ ] No test-split subject data was loaded for any prior experiment.
231	- [ ] All ablations and pilot probes have been run on dev split only.
232	- [ ] Pre-run checklist written to
233	      `30-implement/sleep-staging/runs/pre-run-checklist.txt` before
234	      executing the headline script.
235	
236	**Headline-positive:** The Wilcoxon test rejects H0 (p < 0.05)
237	for the EEG-vs-HRV comparison. Positive result characterizes the
238	EEG advantage; the effect size determines clinical relevance framing
239	(Cases A–C above).
240	
241	**Headline-null:** The Wilcoxon test does not reject H0 (p >= 0.05).
242	Null result means the gap is below the detection threshold at N=77.
243	Per the defensibility-critic analysis: this is an informative null
244	because it bounds the EEG-HRV gap to < ~17 pp at 80 % power (the
245	detection threshold). It directly informs wearable deployment
246	decisions.
247	
248	**Informative regardless of direction:**
249	- The 3-class macro-F1 absolute values for both models (establishes
250	  the performance floor for this population).
